author: śrī⋅śaṅkarācārya
verses:
kadācit kālindī⋅taṭa⋅vipina⋅saṅgīta⋅taralo
mudābhīrī⋅nārī⋅vadana⋅kamalāsvāda⋅madhupaḥ
ramā⋅śambhu⋅brahma⋅marapati⋅gaṇeśārcitapado
jagannāthaḥ svāmī nayanapatha⋅gāmī bhavatu me

bhuje savye veṇuṁ śirasi śikhi⋅picchaṁ kaṭitaṭe
dukūlaṁ netrānte sahacari⋅kaṭākṣaṁ vidadhate
sadā śrīmad⋅vṛndāvana⋅vasati⋅līlā⋅paricayo
jagannāthaḥ svāmī nayanapatha⋅gāmī bhavatu me

mahāmbhodhes⋅tīre kanaka⋅rucire nīla⋅śikhare
vasan prāsādāntaḥ sahaja⋅balabhadreṇa balinā
subhadrā⋅madhyasthaḥ sakala⋅sura⋅sevāvasarado
jagannāthaḥ svāmī nayanapatha⋅gāmī bhavatu me

kṛpā⋅pārāvāraḥ sajala⋅jalada⋅śreṇi⋅ruciro
ramā⋅vāṇī⋅rāmaḥ sphuradamala⋅paṅkeruha⋅mukhaḥ
surendrairārādhyaḥ śrutigaṇaśikhā gītacarito
jagannāthaḥ svāmī nayanapatha⋅gāmī bhavatu me

rathārūḍho gacchan pathi milita⋅bhūdeva⋅paṭalaiḥ
stuti⋅prādurbhāvaṁ pratipadamupākarṇya sadayaḥ
dayāsindhurbandhuḥ sakala jagatāṁ sindhu⋅sutayā
jagannāthaḥ svāmī nayanapatha⋅gāmī bhavatu me

paraṁbrahmāpīḍaḥ kuvalaya⋅dalotphulla⋅nayano
nivāsī nīlādrau nihita⋅caraṇo‛nanta⋅śirasi
rasānandī rādhā⋅sarasa⋅vapurāliṅgana⋅sukho
jagannāthaḥ svāmī nayanapatha⋅gāmī bhavatu me

na vai jāce rājyaṁ na ca kanaka⋅māṇikya⋅vibhavaṁ
na jāce‛haṁ ramyāṁ sakala⋅jana⋅kāmyāṁ varavadhūm
sadā kāle kāle pramatha⋅patinā gīta⋅carito
jagannāthaḥ svāmī nayanapatha⋅gāmī bhavatu me

hara tvaṁ saṁsāraṁ drutatarama-sāraṁ sura⋅pate!
hara tvaṁ pāpānāṁ vitatima-parāṁ jādava⋅pate!
aho dīne‛nāthe nihita⋅caraṇo niścitamidaṁ
jagannāthaḥ svāmī nayanapatha⋅gāmī bhavatu me

jagannāthāṣṭakaṁ puṇyaṁ
jaḥ paṭhet prayataḥ śuciḥ
sarva⋅pāpa⋅viśuddhātmā
viṣṇulokaṁ sa gacchati
en_translation:
Sometimes He is vibrant with music in the forest groves on the banks of the Yamunā, a bee joyfully relishing the lotus-like faces of the cowherd women; whose feet are worshiped by Lakṣmī, Śiva, Brahmā, Indra, and Gaṇeśa—may that Jagannātha, the Lord of the Universe, be the object of my vision.

With a flute on His left arm, a peacock feather on His head, a fine garment around His waist, and the sidelong glances of His companions at the corners of His eyes; He is ever famous for His pastimes of residing in glorious Vṛndāvana—may that Jagannātha, the Lord of the Universe, be the object of my vision.

Residing within His temple on the golden, beautiful Blue Peak on the shore of the great ocean, with His strong elder brother Balabhadra; He stands with Subhadrā in the middle, granting the opportunity for service to all the gods—may that Jagannātha, the Lord of the Universe, be the object of my vision.

He is an ocean of mercy, charming like a fresh cloud; the beloved of Lakṣmī and Sarasvatī, with a glowing, pure lotus-like face; He is worshiped by the chief of the gods, and His glories are sung by the highest Vedic texts—may that Jagannātha, the Lord of the Universe, be the object of my vision.

Riding on His chariot and proceeding along the path, He is met by groups of priests; He graciously hears the manifestation of their praise at every step; He is an ocean of compassion, a friend to all the worlds, accompanied by Lakṣmī—may that Jagannātha, the Lord of the Universe, be the object of my vision.

He is the crown jewel of the Supreme Absolute, with eyes wide open like blooming blue lotus petals; He resides on the Blue Mountain, placing His feet on the head of the cosmic serpent; He experiences the joy of divine mellows, blissful from embracing the charming form of Rādhā—may that Jagannātha, the Lord of the Universe, be the object of my vision.

I do not pray for a kingdom, nor for the opulence of gold and jewels; I do not pray for a beautiful bride desired by all; His glories are always sung by Śiva in every age—may that Jagannātha, the Lord of the Universe, be the object of my vision.

O Lord of the gods! Quickly remove this worthless material existence! O Lord of the Yādavas! Remove the vast multitude of my sins! Oh, it is certain that His feet are placed on the helpless and destitute—may that Jagannātha, the Lord of the Universe, be the object of my vision.

One who recites these eight sacred verses on Jagannātha with devotion and purity, with a soul purified from all sins, goes to the abode of Viṣṇu.
